title: Optimal Error Budgeting for Heterogeneous Sketch Pipelines in Approximate Stream Processing
description: We propose SketchBudget, a framework for analyzing error propagation through pipelines of heterogeneous probabilistic data structures (Bloom filters, Count-Min Sketches, HyperLogLog) and optimally distributing a memory budget across pipeline stages to minimize end-to-end error. Our tight composition bounds and allocation algorithm achieve 20-50% lower error than baselines for the same memory budget.
motivation: Modern stream processing systems compose multiple probabilistic data structures in sequential pipelines, but treat each sketch's error independently. This leads to loose error bounds (from conservative union bounds) and suboptimal memory allocation (uniform or ad-hoc). No existing work formally models how errors propagate through sequential pipelines of heterogeneous sketch types or provides optimal cross-stage memory allocation.
proposed_approach: Three components: (1) An error propagation algebra that models how false positive rates, additive errors, and multiplicative errors compose through pipeline operators (filter, threshold, aggregate). (2) Tight closed-form composition bounds for canonical pipeline patterns (Filter-Estimate, Estimate-Threshold-Count, Filter-Estimate-Aggregate) that exploit cross-stage independence. (3) A greedy allocation algorithm with provable approximation guarantees that distributes a total memory budget across stages to minimize end-to-end error.

related_work.title: An Improved Data Stream Summary: The Count-Min Sketch and its Applications
related_work.relevance: Foundational frequency estimation sketch. We extend their error analysis to account for upstream filtering errors in pipelines.

related_work.title: Sketch-Based Multi-Query Processing over Data Streams
related_work.authors: Alin Dobra, Minos Garofalakis, Johannes Gehrke, Rajeev Rastogi
related_work.relevance: Closest prior work: optimizes memory allocation across sketches for parallel queries. We differ by analyzing sequential pipelines of heterogeneous sketch types with error propagation.

related_work.title: Learning-Augmented Count-Min Sketches via Bayesian Nonparametrics
related_work.authors: Emanuele Dolera, Stefano Favaro, Stefano Peluchetti
related_work.relevance: Improves single-sketch estimation using Bayesian priors. Complementary to our work on multi-sketch pipeline optimization.

related_work.title: HyperLogLog: The Analysis of a Near-Optimal Cardinality Estimation Algorithm
related_work.authors: Philippe Flajolet, Eric Fusy, Olivier Gandouet, Frederic Meunier
related_work.relevance: Foundational cardinality estimation sketch used as a pipeline component in our Estimate-Threshold-Count pattern.

related_work.title: Space/Time Trade-offs in Hash Coding with Allowable Errors
related_work.relevance: Introduced the Bloom filter. We analyze how its false positive rate propagates to downstream pipeline stages.

related_work.title: Mnemosyne: Dynamic Workload-Aware BF Tuning via Accurate Statistics in LSM Trees
related_work.authors: Zichen Zhu, Yanpeng Wei, Ju Hyoung Mun, Manos Athanassoulis
related_work.relevance: Optimizes single Bloom filter sizing based on workload. We extend optimization to multi-sketch pipelines.

related_work.title: The Case for Learned Index Structures
related_work.authors: Tim Kraska, Alex Beutel, Ed H. Chi, Jeffrey Dean, Neoklis Polyzotis
related_work.relevance: Sparked interest in ML-augmented data structures. Learned sketches can be pipeline components whose error we analyze.

related_work.title: A Model for Learned Bloom Filters, and Optimizing by Sandwiching
related_work.relevance: Formalized learned Bloom filter error models. Our framework can incorporate these as drop-in components.

related_work.title: Space-Efficient Online Computation of Quantile Summaries
related_work.relevance: Quantile sketch usable as a pipeline component for threshold-based filtering stages.

related_work.title: Multi-Query Optimization for Sketch-Based Estimation
related_work.authors: Alin Dobra, Minos Garofalakis, Johannes Gehrke, Rajeev Rastogi
related_work.relevance: Journal extension of the 2004 EDBT paper with NP-hardness results for space allocation. Limited to homogeneous sketches.

hypothesis: Composition-aware error analysis and memory allocation across a pipeline of heterogeneous probabilistic data structures will achieve at least 20% lower end-to-end error compared to independent per-stage sizing for the same total memory budget, with the improvement increasing for longer pipelines and more skewed data distributions.
success_criteria: Composition bounds are provably and empirically tighter than naive product/union bounds for all three canonical pipeline configurations
SketchBudget allocation achieves >= 20% error reduction over the best baseline for at least 2 of 3 pipeline configurations
Memory savings of >= 25% for a fixed accuracy target across at least 2 of 3 pipelines
Greedy allocation heuristic runs in under 1 second for pipelines with up to 10 stages
Results are consistent across Zipfian and real-world (CAIDA) data distributions

feasibility.compute: CPU-only; all experiments involve lightweight sketch operations on streaming data. 2 cores sufficient.
feasibility.memory: Well within 128GB; sketches use KB-MB scale memory. Full datasets under 10GB.
feasibility.time: Estimated 4-6 hours for all experiments across 3 pipeline configurations, 5 allocation strategies, multiple distributions, and 5 random seeds each.
feasibility.data: Synthetic Zipfian streams (generated) + publicly available CAIDA traces.
